scope: Iraq model input brief for team_profile only
notes: Model input only; no score, W/D/L, group advancement, champion prediction, or betting advice.

baseline_strength_model.relative_group_position: market and rating baseline place Iraq below France, Senegal and Norway, but recent warm-ups suggest a non-zero upset-resistance profile.

inputs_to_weight.current_value: +50000 winner, +8000 Group I winner in one snapshot

lambda_xg_sensitivity.reason: Iraq's direct play, box occupation and set-piece threat depend heavily on him.
He is one of the few profiles who can connect under pressure.
Hamstring injury and replacement reduce certainty around wide/attacking depth.

style_modeling_notes.low_block_resistance: Iraq can reduce opponent tempo through compactness, but sustained pressure against elite attackers raises late concession risk.
style_modeling_notes.attack_volume: Model should not overstate open-play shot volume.
style_modeling_notes.set_piece_path: Set pieces and Aymen Hussein aerial threat are important non-open-play paths.
style_modeling_notes.game_state: Early concession is especially damaging because Iraq are less suited to chasing high-tempo matches.
style_modeling_notes.draw_gravity: Higher than raw squad value would imply when Iraq keep match tempo low.

market_calibration.interpretation: Market treats Iraq as a major longshot. Do not erase the gap, but do not round them down to zero because compact underdogs can create low-event variance.

uncertainty_flags: Low squad-value baseline vs recent defensive confidence
Ahmed Yahya injury and rotation impact
